paragraphs.text: “And there was a famine in the land… and Isaac went to Abimelech king of the Philistines in Gerar… And Isaac sowed in that land, and received the same year a hundredfold of barley: and the Lord blessed him. And the man waxed great, and went forward, and grew until he became very great” (Gen 26:1, 12–13). Then Abimelech feared that the sojourner would prevail over him. And with Phicol his commander he comes to Isaac and says to him: “We have seen plainly that the Lord was with thee,” as He was with thy father Abraham; we have understood this both from the fact that thou hast reaped a hundredfold, and from much else; “and we said: Let there be now a covenant between us and thee, that thou wilt do us no hurt now that thou hast become strong, even as we have not been hostile to thee when thou wast still weak.” “And they swore one to another… and they departed in peace” (Gen 26:28–29, 31).
